title: Understanding Gravity: Our Cosmic Glue
summary: Gravity shapes our universe by keeping us grounded on Earth and guiding planets in their orbits. This video unravels the mysteries of gravity, from Newton's apple to Einstein's curved spacetime.

sections.heading: What is Gravity?
sections.body: Gravity is a fundamental force of nature, drawing objects with mass towards each other. It's what gives weight to physical objects and causes the tides.
key_points: Attracts two masses toward one another

sections.heading: How Does Gravity Work?
sections.body: Gravity works through the exchange of energy between objects with mass. Sir Isaac Newton formulated this concept, while Albert Einstein later refined it by showing gravity as a warping effect on spacetime.

sections.heading: Why Does Gravity Matter?
sections.body: Understanding gravity is crucial for space exploration and technology. It affects everything from the flight of a basketball to the structure of galaxies, making it pivotal in physics.

key_facts: Newton's Universal Law of Gravitation: F= G * (m1*m2) / r^2
